author: lumoe
description: Rollover any unchecked checkboxes from your last daily note into today's note.

manifest.author: Lukas Mölschl
manifest.description: This Obsidian.md plugin rolls over incomplete TODOs from the previous daily note to today's daily note. (https://obsidian.md). (Originally created by Matthew Sessions)
manifest.name: Rollover Daily Todos

name: Rollover Daily Todos

releases.author: lumoe
releases.description:
## What's Changed
* Adds customizable "done" status markers and do some cleanup. by @ErikaRS in https://github.com/lumoe/obsidian-rollover-daily-todos/pull/159

## New Contributors
* @ErikaRS made their first contribution in https://github.com/lumoe/obsidian-rollover-daily-todos/pull/159

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/lumoe/obsidian-rollover-daily-todos/compare/1.1.8...1.2.0

releases.author: lumoe
releases.description:
## What's Changed
* Support all bullet symbols as per MD spec by @adityavm in https://github.com/lumoe/obsidian-rollover-daily-todos/pull/117
* Support alternative checkboxes by @tobiasvl in https://github.com/lumoe/obsidian-rollover-daily-todos/pull/76

## New Contributors
* @adityavm made their first contribution in https://github.com/lumoe/obsidian-rollover-daily-todos/pull/117
* @tobiasvl made their first contribution in https://github.com/lumoe/obsidian-rollover-daily-todos/pull/76

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/lumoe/obsidian-rollover-daily-todos/compare/1.1.7...1.1.8

releases.author: lumoe
releases.description:
Adds the option for disabling the automatic rollover after file creation. 

Thank you for contributing @barryvan
releases.name: Add setting to disable automatic rollover

releases.author: lumoe
releases.description: Fixes bug in complex date formats https://github.com/lumoe/obsidian-rollover-daily-todos/issues/85

releases.author: lumoe
releases.description: Fix a bug that was caused in "Periodic notes".

releases.author: lumoe
releases.description:
Revert from previous "fix" and allow trailing and preceding slashes in the directory. 

Sorry for the inconvenience. :heart:

releases.author: lumoe
releases.description: Fix bug when constructing the path

releases.author: lumoe
releases.description:
This is the first release after the transfer from @shichongrui - thank you for creating this project in the first place and letting us continue work on it. 

**Changes from 1.0.5:**
- It is possible to have paths in your date format for the daily files. E.g. the format `YYYY/MMM/YYYY-MM-DD` will now also successfully roll over the Todos from the previous day. 

Thanks to @ovo-saxon-zerbino for his contribution in this release.

releases.author: KjellConnelly
releases.description:
**1.0.5 is the same as 1.0.4 except:**
- README was display poorly in Obsidian, so had to create a new release.

**Changes from 1.0.4:**
1. Fixed some bugs that made plugin not work at all on mobile.
2. Added `Periodic Notes` as a possible alternative to `Daily notes`
3. Added new Settings (delete todos after rollover, and ignore empty todos)
4. New command (accessible from Command Palette) to run rollover (as opposed to only when new files are created).
5. New command (accessible from Command Palette) to undo last rollover (only available for 2 minutes since last rollover, and only can be done one time). Just in case the rollover modifies in a way you don't like.
6. Added new `Notice`s to tell the user what rollover has done (# of todos rolled over, and # of empty todos removed)
7. Improved `README.md` to give users a more complete picture of what the plugin does.
8. Todos are rolled over to bottom of file when a `Template Heading` is chosen from `Settings`, but no heading with that name can be found today's daily note.
9. Todos no longer rollover to that last daily note you have created. They only roll over to **Today's Daily Note** (based off your device's date).

releases.author: KjellConnelly
releases.description:
1. Fixed some bugs that made plugin not work at all on mobile.
2. Added `Periodic Notes` as a possible alternative to `Daily notes`
3. Added new Settings (delete todos after rollover, and ignore empty todos)
4. New command (accessible from Command Palette) to run rollover (as opposed to only when new files are created).
5. New command (accessible from Command Palette) to undo last rollover (only available for 2 minutes since last rollover, and only can be done one time). Just in case the rollover modifies in a way you don't like.
6. Added new `Notice`s to tell the user what rollover has done (# of todos rolled over, and # of empty todos removed)
7. Improved `README.md` to give users a more complete picture of what the plugin does.
8. Todos are rolled over to bottom of file when a `Template Heading` is chosen from `Settings`, but no heading with that name can be found today's daily note.
9. Todos no longer rollover to that last daily note you have created. They only roll over to **Today's Daily Note** (based off your device's date).
